$comment: Machine-readable sidecar for Diagnostic_State.md. Written/updated by the system alongside the human-facing state file. Consumed by /start resume gate, revision coach, and state gardening.
$comment_complexity: Adaptive Mid-Run Mode Escalation (docs/adaptive-mode-escalation.md): Tier-1 signals Pass 0/1 record so `validate.sh escalation-check` can recommend a mode escalation before Tier 2. Omit fields not yet discovered (escalation-check reports them unevaluable, never firing). tier1_finding_count is computed from the ledger, not stored.
$comment_synthesis_coverage: Synthesis Coverage Disclosure, M1 (docs/synthesis-regrounding.md): filled at the Synthesis Coverage Manifest step (run-synthesis.md §Processing Protocol step 9b) on every run that writes a full editorial letter — the sidecar projection of the [Project]_Synthesis_Read_Manifest_[runlabel].md written before the letter. coverage must equal the letter's <!-- coverage: ok|degraded --> marker; the tallies must equal the manifest rows; provenance = dispatch-derived (sequential/hybrid/swarm) or declared (single-agent only). verification_excerpt_count is multi-agent only (null single-agent); estimated_context_utilization is single-agent only (null multi-agent). Audited by validate.sh synthesis-coverage; leave this stub untouched on runs without a full letter (an unfilled stub reads as absent).
$comment_structured: Phase 3 — optional machine-readable arrays mirroring the embedded apodictic:* blocks in ledger/letter artifacts. Validated by scripts/structured_findings.py (validate.sh structured-findings). When findings[] is populated, its severities must tally to triage_summary; omit any array to keep older sidecars valid.
$comment_execution: Runner-Governed Execution state (Phase: Harness Engineering, Increment 5). `gate_events` is the canonical append-only gate-decision log (apodictic.gate_event.v1); `phase` (gate frontier), `allowed_next`, `pending_gate` (omitted when none), and `finding_states` are a recomputable resume pointer == fold(gate_events). `state_version: 2` selects this model; the deprecated `gates`/`attested` maps are read only when state_version < 2. Updated by `validate.sh gate <phase> <run_folder>` + `gate --attest`; validate with `validate.sh gate-state`. See docs/runner-governed-execution.md + schemas/execution-gates.v1.json + apodictic.gate_event.v1.schema.json.
$comment_finding_dispositions: Finding dispositions (docs/finding-dispositions.md): `execution.finding_dispositions` maps F-id -> apodictic.finding_disposition.v1 record — the author's declined/deferred decision, an OVERLAY beside finding_states, never a new lifecycle state. The pinned markers (`<!-- declined: F-… — reason -->` / `<!-- deferred: F-… until: trigger — reason -->`) are the canonical source; this map is the mechanical mirror. DUAL-WRITER RULE (mirrors finding_states 'revised'): on a runner-governed project (non-empty gate_events) dispositions ride the revision_round clear as gate_events[].disposition_deltas and this map is fold-derived — never hand-written; on a non-governed project the workflow writes the record directly, validating it (schema + trigger-iff-deferred + non-empty reason) before it lands. SUPERSEDENCE: finding_states[id] == 'revised' supersedes any disposition for id — the record is kept (history), consumers check finding_states first; last-write-wins per id, never deletion. Omit the map to keep older sidecars valid. Audited by `validate.sh disposition-check`.
